---
title: "Mohammed Mudarris"
---
<div style="display:flex; justify-content:flex-end; gap:14px; margin-top:-35px;">

<a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=0kUG_e4AAAAJ&hl=en">
<img src="icons/Google-Scholar.svg" width="22">
</a>

<a href="https://osf.io/e834b/">
<img src="icons/OSF.svg" width="22">
</a>

<a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0003-3649-542">
<img src="icons/ORCID.svg" width="22">
</a>

<a href="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Mohammed-Mudarris">
<img src="icons/Researchgate.svg" width="22">
</a>

<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/mamudarris/">
<img src="icons/LinkedIn.svg" width="22">
</a>

<a href="https://bsky.app/profile/mamudarris.bsky.social">
<img src="icons/Bluesky.svg" width="22">
</a>

<a href="mailto:mamudarris@gmail.com">
<img src="icons/Mail.svg" width="22">
</a>

</div>

![](Self_portrait.JPG){width=220px}

PhD Candidate  
Health, Medical, Neuropsychology |  
Institute of Psychology |  
Faculty of Social and Behavioural Sciences |  
Leiden University

My research focuses on neuroplasticity of motor learning with multisensory stimulation in aging and neurological disease. Using multimodal MRI methods, I examine structural, functional, and network-level brain changes associated with motor learning and rehabilitation.  

## Research Interests

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

- Multimodal Neuroimaging  
- Motor learning & cognition  
- Aging and stroke  
- Multisensory stimulation  

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

- Music-based rehabilitation  
- Cognitive-motor interference  
- Hierarchical modelling  

:::

::::

---
